sections.content: Data types specify the type of data that a variable can store. They help the compiler allocate memory efficiently and perform appropriate operations.
A data type defines the kind of value a variable can hold and the amount of memory allocated to it.
The int data type is used to store whole numbers without decimal points.
The char data type is used to store a single character enclosed within single quotes.
The float data type stores decimal values with single precision.
The double data type stores decimal values with higher precision than float.
The void data type represents the absence of any value.
Signed data types can store both positive and negative values.
Unsigned data types store only positive values and zero.

summary: Data types define the type of data stored in variables.
Basic data types include int, char, float, and double.
Derived data types include arrays, pointers, and functions.
User-defined data types include structures, unions, enums, and typedef.
Type modifiers alter the size and range of data types.
